title: -Contract: DC Electric for On-Call Electrical Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any
amendments with DC Electric to provide on-call electrical services for a three-year
period from December 1, 2025 to November 30, 2028, in an amount not to exceed
$375,000, with an option to extend for two additional one-year terms. The service rates
will be adjusted annually in accordance with the pricing outlined in the awarded bid.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are funds available in the adopted FY2026 Public Works Budget (Funds 127,
134, and 142) for electrical on-call services. Funding for future years is subject to
Council approval of the funds in the Fiscal Years 2027-2028 Budget.
sections.current_situation:
Public Works’ existing contracts for on-call electrical services are older than five years
and set to expire at the end of fiscal year 2026. To avoid disruption in maintenance,
repair and installations for the City’s electrical infrastructure, it is necessary to execute a
new contract based on the results of the recent competitive solicitation.
On-call electrical services provides support on critical electrical projects and tasks,
including:
 Traffic and Pedestrian Signal Installation, Maintenance and Repair
 Conduit Repairs and Support of Electric Vehicle Charges in City-Owned Garages
 Parking, Parks, Pathway and Street Light Installation, Maintenance and Repair
 Indoor Electrical Services and Repairs
The City conducted Invitation to Bid Specification No. 25-11728-C, released on August
12, 2025, with proposals due no later than September 25, 2025. The City received five

Contract: DC Electric for On-Call Electric Services CONSENT CALENDAR

proposals in response. The selection committee, composed of subject matter experts
from Public Works evaluated all proposals.
The evaluation criteria used to rate the proposals were as follows:
 Project Approach (20%),
 Expertise and References (25%),
 Cost (25%), and
 Ability to Perform Services Listed in the Scope of Services (30%).
DC Electric was selected as one of the most responsive bidders to the solicitation.
Additional vendors are anticipated to be selected once the reference check process is
completed.
This contract will support the City’s facilities and is in alignment with the City’s Strategic
Plan, advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
Public Works maintains the City’s electrical infrastructure, which includes 223 traffic
signals and 8,000 streetlights, along internal electrical systems within City facilities.
These systems are essential systems to provide needed traffic control, illumination and
access to electricity for staff and citizens. However, Public Works does not have the
staffing capacity and expertise required to support all the needs of the City’s electrical
systems. These systems need regular maintenance and repair to run optimally and
function efficiently. Public Works also lacks the staffing capacity required to install or
upgrade lighting and electrical systems, so the Department utilizes vendors for support.
DC Electric responded to the bid invitation and were selected as a responsive bidder.
The selected vendor has the adequate staff and equipment necessary to service the
City. This contract will ensure clear guidelines and standardized prices for electrical
services for City assets.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Regular maintenance and repair of electrical systems is essential for optimizing system
performance, prolonging equipment lifespan and minimizing the environmental impact
of these systems. Well-maintained electrical systems operate more efficiently, which
leads to consuming less energy and reducing carbon emissions.
sections.rationale:
The City requires reliable and responsive electrical services to maintain the electrical
infrastructure and assets of City facilities. This agreement ensures ongoing service
and prevents potential disruption to City operations.
Page 2
Page 3 of 6
Contract: DC Electric for On-Call Electric Services CONSENT CALENDAR
November 18, 2025
The on-call electrical services contract will address the City’s needs, while stabilizing
costs and fees. The selected service provider will be utilized as needed to ensure the
City has adequate resources available for electrical installation, maintenance and
repair.
sections.alternatives:
If Public Works does not contract for electrical services, there will be delays and
increased costs associated with maintaining and updating the City’s electrical
infrastructure.

Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: DC ELECTRIC FOR ON-CALL ELECTRICAL SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Solicitation No. 25-11728-C with bids due no later
than September 25, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the City received five proposals and a selection committee, composed of
subject matter experts from the Department of Public Works , evaluated the bids; and
WHEREAS, DC Electric was selected as one of the most responsive and highest-
scoring vendors; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY2026 adopted budget in the Public Works
Electrical Division Fund 127, Fund 134 and Fund 142 budgets. Funding for future years
is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed citywide budget for Fiscal Years 2027-
2028.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with DC Electric to
provide on-call electrical services for Public Works Electrical Division for a three-year
period from December 1, 2025 to November 30, 2028 in an amount not to exceed
$375,000, with an option to extend for two additional one-year terms. The service rates
will be adjusted annually in accordance with the pricing outlined in the awarded bid.
